q: Among the thinkers in this hub, who argues most directly against Stoic detachment, and on what grounds? Ground it in a corpus.
final:
I can’t answer this as asked from the hub’s material that I’m able to retrieve right now.

- In the hub, I do find notes that oppose a *Stoic-style* stance toward suffering/detachment, but the specific thinker-and-grounds formulation (“most directly against Stoic detachment” and “on what grounds”) is not backed by retrievable text in the knowledge base: when I try to open the relevant pages/sections, the returned content is empty (e.g., attempts to load Nietzsche “chains/stradanie_kak_material.md” and “principles/stradanie_vozvyshaet.md”, and the English “en/topics/stradanie.md” return empty text).
- Also, attempts to target a Stoicism-specific corpus fail (“Federation is not configured for kb_id "stoicism".”).

What I *can* verify from the search index snippets (without being able to open the full notes) is that there is at least one directly relevant claim in a hub entry on suffering. For example, the search result snippet for **“Suffering” (en/topics/stradanie.md)** includes this line:
> “suffering is always the consequence of wrong thought; circumstance merely reveals to a man his own character.”  
But because I can’t open the note body, I can’t reliably determine which thinker in the hub it is attributing this to, nor the full grounds of the argument.

If you want, tell me which hub thinkers you mean (or paste the relevant passage), and I’ll verify the “most direct” opposition and grounds against Stoic detachment using only what I can retrieve.
